question: [File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TokenAirdropService.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getAirdrops()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the TokenAirdropMapper.mapToNullIfZero() method, which maps serialNumber=0 to null in the API response, to infer that a returned airdrop is a fungible token (not NFT) staking reward, and use this information to prioritize high-value fungible token reward theft targets?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TokenAirdropService.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getAirdrops()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the EXTRACTOR function in TokenAirdropsController that maps serialNumber to '0L' string when null, causing the pagination next link to include serialnumber=0 which may be interpreted differently by the Bound parser than intended, leading to incorrect pagination that skips or duplicates pending staking reward airdrop records?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TokenAirdropService.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getAirdrops()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the getSort() method in TokenAirdropsController, which checks lastSerial == DEFAULT_SERIAL_VALUE (0L) to decide whether to include SERIAL_NUMBER in the sort, to craft a request where the last returned airdrop has serialNumber=0, causing the next pagination link to omit serial number and expose additional pending staking reward airdrops in subsequent pages?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TokenAirdropService.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getAirdrops()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the LinkFactoryImpl.isSameDirection() method's string prefix matching (checking for 'gt:' or 'gte:' for ASC order) to craft a parameter value like 'GTE:1000' (uppercase) that is not recognized as same-direction, causing the original upper bound to be retained in the next link alongside the new lower bound, creating an unintended range that exposes additional pending staking reward airdrops?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TokenAirdropService.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getAirdrops()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the LinkFactoryImpl.containsEq() method's hasEq() logic (which treats any value not starting with gt:/gte:/lt:/lte: as EQ) to inject a crafted parameter value that is misclassified as an equality constraint, causing the pagination link generator to skip adding a new bound and exposing pending staking reward airdrops from a previous page?
